title: Ensure tab completion works with installed brew package
description: Verify and ensure that shell tab completion works correctly when dogcat is installed via Homebrew (brew). This includes testing that completions are properly registered and functional for the dcat CLI.

title: Add worktree field to Issue data model
description: Add an optional worktree field to the Issue dataclass in models.py to track which git worktree an issue belongs to. This would allow assigning and filtering issues by worktree when multiple worktrees share the same .dogcats directory.

title: Set up dcat web command with FastAPI + Jinja2 + Tailwind skeleton
description: Create the dcat web subcommand (feature-gated behind DCAT_FEATURE_WEB). Set up FastAPI app with Jinja2 templating and Tailwind CSS via CDN. Bind to 127.0.0.1:8000 by default. Add FastAPI and Jinja2 as optional dependencies. Create base template with Tailwind CDN link, navigation layout, and project name header.
acceptance:
- dcat web starts a FastAPI server on 127.0.0.1:8000
- Feature-gated: requires DCAT_FEATURE_WEB=1
- Base HTML template with Tailwind CDN and nav layout
- / redirects to /issues
- --port and --host flags supported
notes: Mobile-first responsive design. Use Tailwind responsive utilities (sm/md/lg breakpoints). Primarily tested on mobile viewports.

title: Issue list view
description: Read-only issue list page showing all issues in a filterable table. Reuse storage.py to load issues. Support filtering by status, type, priority, and labels via query params. Show columns: ID, title, type, status, priority, labels, updated_at.
acceptance:
- /issues route renders a list of all issues
- Filters for status, type, priority, labels
- Sortable by priority and updated_at
- Links to issue detail view
notes: Mobile-friendly layout: stack filters vertically on small screens, compact table or card-based list on mobile.

title: Kanban board view
description: Read-only kanban board showing issues as cards grouped by status columns (Open, In Progress, In Review, Blocked, Deferred). Each card shows issue ID, title, type badge, and priority indicator. Cards link to issue detail view.
acceptance:
- /kanban route renders a kanban board
- Columns for each active status
- Issue cards with key info
- Cards link to detail view
- Responsive layout with horizontal scroll if needed
notes: Mobile-friendly kanban: consider vertical column stacking or horizontal swipe on small screens.

title: Issue detail view
description: Read-only detail page for a single issue. Shows all fields: title, description, status, priority, type, labels, owner, acceptance criteria, design notes, notes, comments, dependencies (blocks/blocked-by), parent/children, external ref, timestamps. Render markdown in description and notes fields.
acceptance:
- /issues/{issue_id} route renders full issue details
- All issue fields displayed
- Dependencies shown with links to related issues
- Parent/children shown with links
- Comments listed chronologically
- Markdown rendered in text fields
notes: Mobile-friendly detail layout: single-column, readable on small screens.

title: Dependency graph visualization
description: Visual dependency graph showing issue relationships. Consider using a JS graph library (e.g., D3, dagre, mermaid) to render blocks/blocked-by and parent/child relationships.

title: History/timeline view
description: View showing issue change history and timeline. Reuse event log data from storage to show a chronological feed of issue changes across the project.

title: Dashboard/summary stats view
description: Dashboard page with summary statistics: issue counts by status, type, priority. Burn-down style metrics. Overview of project health.

title: Replace Tailwind CDN with standalone CLI build
description: Replace Tailwind CSS CDN link with the standalone Tailwind CLI for offline support and smaller CSS output. Bundle the compiled CSS with the package.

title: File-watching event stream library
description: Internal library that watches issues.jsonl for changes and emits events. Uses watchdog for filesystem monitoring. Exposes a Python API (not CLI) that consumers (web UI, future CLI command) can import. Lives in src/dogcat/stream/ or similar.

title: Read-write support for Web UI
description: Add write capabilities to the Web UI: create issues, update fields, change status, close issues, add comments. Requires CSRF protection and form validation. Reuse storage.py write methods.

title: Multi-project support in Web UI
description: Support loading .dogcats/ folders from multiple repositories simultaneously. Add a project switcher in the nav. Single web server instance serves all configured projects. Configuration for project paths (CLI args or config file).

title: Wire live updates into Web UI via SSE
description: Add SSE endpoint to the web server that uses the internal file-watching stream library (dogcat-3tuf) to push issue change events to the browser. Add JS client-side listener to auto-refresh views without polling. Should import the library directly, not shell out to a CLI command.

title: Web UI test infrastructure and tests
description: Set up test infrastructure for the web UI using httpx AsyncClient as the FastAPI test client. Write tests for all routes: /, /issues, /issues/{id}, /kanban. Test filtering, sorting, error handling (missing issues, invalid filters). Test template rendering produces expected HTML structure. Add to the existing pytest suite.
acceptance:
- Test client fixture using httpx AsyncClient
- Route tests for all endpoints
- Filter and sort parameter tests
- Error case tests (404 for missing issues, bad params)
- Template content assertions
- Integrated into just test

Designed for desktop and mobile, with a mobile-first approach — primarily tested on mobile. Initial version is read-only. Future phases: read-write support, multi-project support, live updates.

Fix -n short flag conflict between --limit and --namespace
description:
The -n short flag is used for --limit in recently-closed and recently-added, but -n is reserved for --namespace (per the -n shorthand proposal dogcat-oq90). This creates an inconsistency across the CLI.

Affected commands using -n for --limit:
- recently-closed (src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1215)
- recently-added (src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1281)

Additionally, -n is also overloaded for:
- --notes in create (_cmd_create.py:153) and update (_cmd_update.py:85)
- --dry-run in prune (_cmd_admin.py:34)

Fix: Remove -n as a short flag from --limit in recently-closed and recently-added. Consider also auditing --notes and --dry-run usages of -n for consistency.

Add dcat git rebase command to auto-resolve JSONL merge conflicts
description:
Add a `dcat git rebase` command that detects and auto-resolves merge conflicts in `.dogcats/*.jsonl` files (issues.jsonl and inbox.jsonl) using the existing semantic merge logic in merge_driver.py.

**Behavior:**
1. Scan `.dogcats/issues.jsonl` and `.dogcats/inbox.jsonl` for git conflict markers (`<<<<<<<`, `=======`, `>>>>>>>`)
2. Extract base/ours/theirs sections from the conflicted file
3. Run `merge_jsonl()` to produce a clean resolved result
4. Write the resolved file and `git add` it
5. If no conflicts found, print a message and exit

**Does NOT perform any git operations** (no pull, no rebase, no continue) — it only resolves the JSONL conflicts. The user handles the git workflow themselves.

**Typical workflow:**
```
git pull --rebase
# conflict in .dogcats/issues.jsonl
dcat git rebase
# resolved
git rebase --continue
```
acceptance:
- Resolves conflict markers in issues.jsonl using semantic merge logic
- Resolves conflict markers in inbox.jsonl using semantic merge logic
- Runs git add on resolved files
- Reports which files were resolved
- Reports when no conflicts are found
- Has tests

Fix -n short flag conflict between --limit and --namespace
description:
The -n short flag is used for --limit in recently-closed and recently-added, but -n is reserved for --namespace (per the -n shorthand proposal dogcat-oq90). This creates an inconsistency across the CLI.

Affected commands using -n for --limit:
- recently-closed (src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1215)
- recently-added (src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py:1281)

Additionally, -n is also overloaded for:
- --notes in create (_cmd_create.py:153) and update (_cmd_update.py:85)
- --dry-run in prune (_cmd_admin.py:34)

Fix: Remove -n as a short flag from --limit in recently-closed and recently-added. Consider also auditing --notes and --dry-run usages of -n for consistency.

dcat doctor: detect and fix missing Claude Code hooks (PreCompact/SessionStart)
description:
After context compaction, agents lose workflow context and become braindead. Claude Code supports PreCompact and SessionStart hooks that can re-inject workflow context.

**What**: Add a dcat doctor check that:
1. Detects if .claude/settings.json (or settings.local.json) exists
2. Checks whether PreCompact and SessionStart hooks are registered to run `dcat prime`
3. If missing, warns the user and offers to install them automatically

**Hook config to install** (in .claude/settings.json or settings.local.json):
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [{
      "matcher": "",
      "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "dcat prime" }]
    }],
    "PreCompact": [{
      "matcher": "",
      "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "dcat prime" }]
    }]
  }
}
```

**Why**: beads does exactly this — `bd prime` on both SessionStart and PreCompact. The PreCompact hook injects context right before the compactor summarizes, so workflow instructions survive compaction. SessionStart re-injects after compaction completes.

**Acceptance criteria**:
- dcat doctor reports missing hooks as a warning (not a failure — not all users use Claude Code)
- When hooks are missing, doctor asks if user wants to fix it
- Fix installs hooks into .claude/settings.local.json (not committed to repo) if it exists, otherwise .claude/settings.json
- Merges with existing hooks/settings rather than overwriting
- If hooks are already present, check passes silently
- Works whether dcat is installed globally or via uv

dcat doctor: detect and fix missing Claude Code PreCompact hook
description:
After context compaction, agents lose workflow context and become braindead. Claude Code supports a PreCompact hook that injects context right before the compactor summarizes, so workflow instructions survive compaction.

**What**: Add a dcat doctor check that:
1. Detects if .claude/ directory exists (skip check entirely if not — not all users use Claude Code)
2. Checks whether a PreCompact hook is registered to run `dcat prime` in settings.json or settings.local.json
3. If missing, warns the user and offers to install it automatically

**Hook config to install**:
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PreCompact": [{
      "matcher": "",
      "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "dcat prime" }]
    }]
  }
}
```

**Why PreCompact only (no SessionStart)**: dcat is optional — most repos won't have .dogcats/. A SessionStart hook would run on every Claude Code session and error/noise in non-dogcat repos. Session start is already handled by the CLAUDE.md instruction 'always run dcat prime'. PreCompact is the one case CLAUDE.md can't cover, since the agent forgets it after compaction.

**Prior art**: beads does this with `bd prime` on both hooks, but beads is less optional (it owns the project).

**Acceptance criteria**:
- dcat doctor skips the check entirely if .claude/ directory doesn't exist
- Reports missing PreCompact hook as a warning (not a failure)
- When hook is missing, doctor asks if user wants to fix it
- Fix installs hook into .claude/settings.local.json (preferred, gitignored) or .claude/settings.json
- Merges with existing hooks/settings rather than overwriting
- If hook is already present, check passes silently

**What**: Add a dcat doctor check that:
1. Detects if .claude/ directory exists (skip check entirely if not — not all users use Claude Code)
2. Checks whether a PreCompact hook is registered to run `dcat prime` in settings.json or settings.local.json
3. If missing, warns the user and offers to install it automatically

**Hook config to install**:
```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "PreCompact": [{
      "matcher": "",
      "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "dcat prime" }]
    }]
  }
}
```

**Why PreCompact only (no SessionStart)**: dcat is optional — most repos won't have .dogcats/. A SessionStart hook would run on every Claude Code session and error/noise in non-dogcat repos. Session start is already handled by the CLAUDE.md instruction 'always run dcat prime'. PreCompact is the one case CLAUDE.md can't cover, since the agent forgets it after compaction.

**Prior art**: beads does this with `bd prime` on both hooks, but beads is less optional (it owns the project).

**Acceptance criteria**:
- dcat doctor skips the check entirely if .claude/ directory doesn't exist
- Reports missing PreCompact hook as a warning (not a failure)
- When hook is missing, doctor asks if user wants to fix it
- Fix installs hook into .claude/settings.local.json (preferred, gitignored) or .claude/settings.json
- Merges with existing hooks/settings rather than overwriting
- If hook is already present, check passes silently

Agents skip 'create issue first' rule for small/obvious tasks
description: CLAUDE.md explicitly states: 'If the user brings up a new bug, feature or anything else that warrants changes to the code, ALWAYS ask if we should create an issue for it before you start working on the code.' Despite this, agents routinely skip this step when the task feels small or obvious — rationalizing that it's 'just two lines' or 'too trivial for an issue'. This defeats the purpose of the rule, which exists for traceability and discipline regardless of task size. The --opinionated prime output should reinforce this more forcefully. Possible approaches: add a dedicated rule in the opinionated section that explicitly calls out this failure mode, reword to make it harder to rationalize away, or make it a top-level rule rather than buried in prose.

Add exhaustiveness check for unknown record types in classify_record()
description:
classify_record() in src/dogcat/models.py defaults to 'issue' for any unrecognized record_type value. If a new record type is added in the future without updating the merge driver, those records get misclassified and merged with wrong semantics — potentially causing silent data loss during merges.

Fix: Add an exhaustiveness check that warns or raises on unknown record types instead of silently defaulting to 'issue'. The merge driver (src/dogcat/merge_driver.py) relies on classify_record() at lines 207, 223, 251, 271, 297.

Files: src/dogcat/models.py:468-482, src/dogcat/merge_driver.py

Add warning logs for malformed records in merge conflict sections
description:
parse_conflicted_jsonl() in src/dogcat/merge_driver.py uses contextlib.suppress(orjson.JSONDecodeError) with no logging at lines 81 and 96 inside conflict sections. Data can be silently lost during merge with zero trace.

The non-conflict parser _parse_jsonl() already logs warnings for malformed lines (line 129-134) — this should be consistent. Malformed records in conflict sections should produce at least a warning log.

Files: src/dogcat/merge_driver.py:81,96 (conflict sections) vs :129-134 (existing warning pattern)

Fix compaction race condition in storage layer
description:
_maybe_compact() in src/dogcat/storage.py checks line counts outside the lock (lines 417-428), then _save() acquires the lock separately. Two concurrent processes can both decide to compact redundantly.

More critically, _maybe_compact() is called after releasing the append lock (line 394), so line counts may be stale between the append completing and the compaction check running.

Fix: Move the compaction eligibility check inside the lock, or call _maybe_compact() within the append lock context.

Files: src/dogcat/storage.py:394,417-428

title.new: Fix compaction race condition in storage layer

Add optional per-record checksums to JSONL storage
description:
JSONL records in src/dogcat/storage.py have no embedded CRC or hash. A bit-flip or partial write producing valid-but-incorrect JSON is undetectable. dcat doctor can catch structural issues but not data corruption.

Consider adding an optional record-level checksum (e.g., CRC32 trailer) for a stricter validation mode. This would allow detecting corrupted-but-valid JSON records that currently pass parsing without error.

Files: src/dogcat/storage.py

title.new: Add optional per-record checksums to JSONL storage

JSONL records in src/dogcat/storage.py have no embedded CRC or hash. A bit-flip or partial write producing valid-but-incorrect JSON is undetectable. dcat doctor can catch structural issues but not data corruption.

Consider adding an optional record-level checksum (e.g., CRC32 trailer) for a stricter validation mode. This would allow detecting corrupted-but-valid JSON records that currently pass parsing without error.

Add dcat hook install/uninstall/list commands for Claude Code hooks
description:
Add CLI commands to manage Claude Code hooks in .claude/settings.local.json.

## Commands

### dcat hook install <name> [options]
Installs a named hook into .claude/settings.local.json. Creates the file or merges into existing structure. Idempotent — skips with a message if already present.

Available hooks:
- sessionStart: SessionStart event, matcher 'startup|resume|clear', runs 'dcat prime'. With --opinionated flag, runs 'dcat prime --opinionated' instead.
- preCompact: PreCompact event, matcher '' (all), runs 'dcat prime --replay'.

### dcat hook uninstall <name>
Removes the named hook from .claude/settings.local.json. Cleans up empty hooks/event arrays after removal.

### dcat hook list
Shows installed hooks from .claude/settings.local.json. Also shows hooks from .claude/settings.json marked as '(shared)'.

## Edge cases
- No .claude/settings.local.json exists → create it
- File exists but no hooks key → add it
- Hook already installed → no-op with message
- Uninstall hook that isn't installed → no-op with message

## Tab completions
Add autocompletion callbacks for hook names (sessionStart, preCompact) on install and uninstall subcommands, per project conventions.
acceptance:
- [ ] dcat hook install sessionStart adds SessionStart hook to .claude/settings.local.json
- [ ] dcat hook install sessionStart --opinionated uses 'dcat prime --opinionated'
- [ ] dcat hook install preCompact adds PreCompact hook
- [ ] Installs are idempotent
- [ ] dcat hook uninstall removes hooks and cleans up empty structures
- [ ] dcat hook list shows installed hooks (local and shared)
- [ ] Tab completions for hook names on install/uninstall
- [ ] Tests for all commands

title.new: Add dcat hook install/uninstall/list commands for Claude Code hooks

Support per-repo config.local.toml when using shared database via .dogcatrc
description: When .dogcatrc redirects to a shared .dogcats directory, config.local.toml is only read from the shared location. This means all repos sharing a database cannot have their own default namespace or visible_namespaces. Add support for reading a local config.local.toml from the repo's own .dogcats/ directory (or alongside .dogcatrc), merged on top of the shared config. This enables the multi-repo workflow where each repo sets its own namespace and visibility filters while sharing one issues.jsonl.

storage.update() doesn't clear closed_at when status changes away from closed
description:
When using `dcat update --status <non-closed>` on a closed issue, the status field changes but `closed_at`, `close_reason`, and `closed_by` are not cleared. This causes data inconsistency where an issue shows as e.g. 'in_review' but still has a closed_at timestamp, making it appear in both the in-review list and showing a '[closed ...]' annotation.

Root cause: `storage.update()` is a generic field setter that uses `setattr` without any awareness of status transition side effects. The dedicated `reopen()` method correctly clears `closed_at` (line 1043), but `update()` bypasses that logic entirely.

Conversely, setting status to `closed` via `update()` doesn't populate `closed_at` either — only `storage.close()` does.

Fix: add status transition guards in `storage.update()` after the field-setting loop:
- If status changed away from closed → clear closed_at, close_reason, closed_by
- If status changed to closed → set closed_at to now

Files: `src/dogcat/storage.py` (update method ~line 722), tests in `tests/`
acceptance:
1. `dcat update --status in_review <closed-issue>` clears closed_at, close_reason, closed_by
2. `dcat update --status closed <open-issue>` sets closed_at
3. Existing `close()` and `reopen()` behavior unchanged
4. Tests cover both transitions

title.new: storage.update() doesn't clear closed_at when status changes away from closed

description.new:
When using `dcat update --status <non-closed>` on a closed issue, the status field changes but `closed_at`, `close_reason`, and `closed_by` are not cleared. This causes data inconsistency where an issue shows as e.g. 'in_review' but still has a closed_at timestamp, making it appear in both the in-review list and showing a '[closed ...]' annotation.

Root cause: `storage.update()` is a generic field setter that uses `setattr` without any awareness of status transition side effects. The dedicated `reopen()` method correctly clears `closed_at` (line 1043), but `update()` bypasses that logic entirely.

Conversely, setting status to `closed` via `update()` doesn't populate `closed_at` either — only `storage.close()` does.

Fix: add status transition guards in `storage.update()` after the field-setting loop:
- If status changed away from closed → clear closed_at, close_reason, closed_by
- If status changed to closed → set closed_at to now

Files: `src/dogcat/storage.py` (update method ~line 722), tests in `tests/`

Add snooze/postpone feature for issues
description:
Add ability to snooze/postpone issues for a specified duration. Snoozed issues keep their original status (open, in_progress, in_review, etc.) but are hidden from `dcat list` and `dcat ready` output by default.

## Behavior
- Snooze is orthogonal to status — an issue can be snoozed regardless of its current status
- Snoozed issues are excluded from `list` and `ready` unless `--include-snoozed` or `--all` is used
- When the snooze period expires, the issue reappears automatically (no status change needed)
- Users can unsnooze early

## CLI surface
- `dcat snooze <id> <duration>` — snooze for relative duration (7d, 2w, 1m)
- `dcat snooze <id> --until <date>` — snooze until absolute date
- `dcat unsnooze <id>` — clear snooze early
- `dcat snoozed` — list currently snoozed issues with expiry dates
- `dcat update <id> --snooze-until <date>` — alternative via update command

## Data model
- Add `snoozed_until: datetime | None` field to Issue dataclass
- Add to UPDATABLE_FIELDS and TRACKED_FIELDS for audit trail
- Orthogonal to status — no new status enum value needed

## Filtering changes
- `get_ready_work()` in deps.py: exclude issues where snoozed_until > now
- `list` command: exclude snoozed issues from default output
- Add `--include-snoozed` flag to list/ready commands

## Files to touch
- models.py: Add snoozed_until field
- constants.py: Add to TRACKED_FIELDS
- storage.py: Add to UPDATABLE_FIELDS, filtering logic
- deps.py: Exclude snoozed from ready work
- cli/_cmd_read.py: List filtering
- cli/_cmd_workflow.py: New snooze/unsnooze/snoozed commands
- cli/_cmd_update.py: --snooze-until option
- cli/_completions.py: Duration/date completers
- cli/_formatting.py: Snoozed badge in display
- cli/_helpers.py: Common filter updates

- [ ] Expired snoozes automatically unhide (no cron needed)

Upgrade GitHub Actions to Node.js 24
description:
GitHub is deprecating Node.js 20 in Actions runners. Three actions across our 4 workflow files still run on Node.js 20:

- `actions/checkout@v4` -> upgrade to `@v5`
- `astral-sh/setup-uv@v4` -> upgrade to latest major with Node 24 support
- `softprops/action-gh-release@v2` -> no v3 yet; use `FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true` env var as workaround

**Timeline:** Node.js 24 forced default June 2, 2026. Node.js 20 removed September 16, 2026.

**Affected files:**
- .github/workflows/ci.yml
- .github/workflows/publish.yml
- .github/workflows/release.yml
- .github/workflows/validate-issues.yml
acceptance:
- All workflows use Node.js 24-compatible action versions
- No Node.js 20 deprecation warnings in CI runs

Add --manual filter to all list-style subcommands
description:
Currently `dcat list` (and siblings like `ready`, `blocked`, `pr`, `in-progress`, `in-review`, `deferred`, `stale`, `snoozed`, `recently-added`, `recently-closed`, `open`, `search`) support `--agent-only` to hide manual issues, but there is no inverse `--manual` flag to show only manual issues within those same views.

There is a dedicated `dcat manual` subcommand, but it cannot be combined with the semantics of other listing subcommands (e.g. 'manual issues that are also in-review', 'manual issues that are blocked', 'manual issues ready to work').

Add a `--manual` option to every listing subcommand for symmetry with `--agent-only`. When set, only issues marked as manual are shown. `--manual` and `--agent-only` are mutually exclusive.

Scope — apply to these commands (the ones that list multiple issues):
- list
- ready
- blocked
- pr
- in-progress
- in-review
- deferred
- stale
- snoozed
- recently-added
- recently-closed
- open
- search

Helpers live in src/dogcat/cli/_helpers.py; the existing `--agent-only` wiring is a good reference for how to thread this through. Completers in src/dogcat/cli/_completions.py: `--manual` is a boolean flag so no completer is required, but verify tab completion still works for each affected command using tabcomp.py.
acceptance:
- Every listed subcommand accepts `--manual` and returns only issues where `manual=True`.
- `--manual` combined with `--agent-only` raises a clear error (mutually exclusive).
- `--manual` composes with other filters (--status, --type, --priority, --label, --namespace, etc.).
- Tests cover the flag on at least `list`, `ready`, and one of the status-specific commands (e.g. `in-progress`).
- `just lint-all` and `just test-all` pass.

Track metadata field changes in event log and dcat diff
description:
`dcat diff` and `dcat history` do not show changes to fields stored under `issue.metadata` (notably `manual` and `no_agent`). Flipping `--manual` on or off is invisible in the audit log and the diff output, even though it materially affects whether agents will pick up an issue.

## Root cause

`TRACKED_FIELDS` in `src/dogcat/constants.py:159` lists only top-level Issue fields. `metadata` is not included, so:

- `storage._tracked_changes()` (`src/dogcat/storage.py:469`) skips it when emitting events to the event log
- `_cmd_diff.py` (`src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_diff.py:253` and `:330`) only iterates `TRACKED_FIELDS`/`TRACKED_PROPOSAL_FIELDS`

## Proposed fix

Track metadata changes generally rather than special-casing `manual`. Diff the metadata dict and emit per-key changes (e.g. `metadata.manual`, `metadata.no_agent`) so any future metadata flag is automatically covered.

Touch points:
- `src/dogcat/storage.py` `_tracked_changes()` — also compare metadata key-by-key and emit nested changes
- `src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_diff.py` — same comparison logic against old/new states from git
- `src/dogcat/cli/_formatting.py` `format_event()` — render `metadata.<key>` changes readably (verify it does the right thing)

## Acceptance

- Toggling `dcat update <id> --manual` and `--no-manual` (or whatever the inverse is) appears in `dcat diff` and `dcat history`
- Same coverage for `no_agent` if/when it is set
- New tests covering metadata change detection in both the event log and the diff command
acceptance:
- `dcat diff` shows manual flag flips between HEAD and working tree
- `dcat history` records manual/no_agent metadata changes as events
- Tests cover both directions (flip on, flip off) for the storage event emission and the diff command

title: Track metadata field changes in event log and dcat diff
dcat label add/remove emits no event due to in-place list mutation
description:
`dcat label <id> add/remove` does not produce an event in `dcat history` or appear in `dcat diff`, even though the label change is persisted to storage.

## Reproduction

```
dcat create 'x'
dcat label dcat-xxx add -l foo
dcat history   # only the 'created' event — no labels change
```

By contrast, `dcat update --labels foo` works correctly and emits a `labels: [] -> ['foo']` event.

## Root cause

`src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_label.py:70-75` mutates `issue.labels` in place before calling `storage.update()`:

```python
if label_name not in issue.labels:
    issue.labels.append(label_name)            # in-place mutation
    updates: dict[str, Any] = {"labels": issue.labels}
    storage.update(issue_id, updates)
```

Inside `storage.update()` (`src/dogcat/storage.py:719`), the snapshot pattern is:

```python
old_values = {k: getattr(issue, k, None) for k in updates if k in TRACKED_FIELDS}
# ... mutate ...
new_values = {k: getattr(issue, k, None) for k in old_values}
```

Both snapshots take the same already-mutated list reference, so `_tracked_changes()` sees `old == new` and `_emit_event()` returns early on the empty changes dict.

The same shape applies to any list/dict-valued tracked field updated by reference rather than replaced.

## Proposed fix

Defensively copy list/dict values in `storage.update()`'s old-values snapshot so in-place mutations on the live Issue can't defeat the diff. One line in `_tracked_changes()` or at the snapshot site:

```python
def _snapshot(value):
    if isinstance(value, list):
        return list(value)
    if isinstance(value, dict):
        return dict(value)
    return value

old_values = {k: _snapshot(getattr(issue, k, None)) ...}
```

This is preferable to fixing each call site because it makes storage robust against the mistake.

## Acceptance

- `dcat label add/remove` emits an `updated` event with a `labels` change in `dcat history` and `dcat diff`
- `dcat update --labels ...` continues to emit events as before
- Test covering the in-place-mutation case for storage.update() (a list-valued tracked field mutated and passed back, asserting old != new in the emitted event)
acceptance:
- `dcat label <id> add`/`remove` produces a `labels: [...] -> [...]` event visible in `dcat history` and `dcat diff`
- `dcat update --labels` continues to work correctly
- Test asserting that mutating a list field in place and passing it through storage.update() still emits a correct old/new diff

title: dcat label add/remove emits no event due to in-place list mutation
dcat label add/remove emits no event due to in-place list mutation
description:
`dcat label <id> add/remove` does not produce an event in `dcat history` or appear in `dcat diff`, even though the label change is persisted to storage.

## Reproduction

```
dcat create 'x'
dcat label dcat-xxx add -l foo
dcat history   # only the 'created' event — no labels change
```

By contrast, `dcat update --labels foo` works correctly and emits a `labels: [] -> ['foo']` event.

## Root cause

`src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_label.py:70-75` mutates `issue.labels` in place before calling `storage.update()`:

```python
if label_name not in issue.labels:
    issue.labels.append(label_name)            # in-place mutation
    updates: dict[str, Any] = {"labels": issue.labels}
    storage.update(issue_id, updates)
```

Inside `storage.update()` (`src/dogcat/storage.py:719`), the snapshot pattern is:

```python
old_values = {k: getattr(issue, k, None) for k in updates if k in TRACKED_FIELDS}
# ... mutate ...
new_values = {k: getattr(issue, k, None) for k in old_values}
```

Both snapshots take the same already-mutated list reference, so `_tracked_changes()` sees `old == new` and `_emit_event()` returns early on the empty changes dict.

The same shape applies to any list/dict-valued tracked field updated by reference rather than replaced.

## Proposed fix

Defensively copy list/dict values in `storage.update()`'s old-values snapshot so in-place mutations on the live Issue can't defeat the diff. One line in `_tracked_changes()` or at the snapshot site:

```python
def _snapshot(value):
    if isinstance(value, list):
        return list(value)
    if isinstance(value, dict):
        return dict(value)
    return value

old_values = {k: _snapshot(getattr(issue, k, None)) ...}
```

This is preferable to fixing each call site because it makes storage robust against the mistake.

## Acceptance

- `dcat label add/remove` emits an `updated` event with a `labels` change in `dcat history` and `dcat diff`
- `dcat update --labels ...` continues to emit events as before
- Test covering the in-place-mutation case for storage.update() (a list-valued tracked field mutated and passed back, asserting old != new in the emitted event)
acceptance:
- `dcat label <id> add`/`remove` produces a `labels: [...] -> [...]` event visible in `dcat history` and `dcat diff`
- `dcat update --labels` continues to work correctly
- Test asserting that mutating a list field in place and passing it through storage.update() still emits a correct old/new diff

Add --has-comments / --without-comments filter to list commands
description:
Add a pair of mutually-exclusive boolean filters to all list-style commands so users can quickly find issues that have (or lack) comments.

## Flags
- `--has-comments` — show only issues with at least one comment
- `--without-comments` — show only issues with zero comments
- The two are mutually exclusive (raise typer.BadParameter if both are passed, similar to check_agent_manual_exclusive in src/dogcat/cli/_helpers.py)

## Comment semantics
Comments are hard-deleted from `issue.comments` (see src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_comment.py:130). There is no soft-delete on the Comment dataclass, so 'non-deleted comments' is just `bool(issue.comments)`. If a soft-delete is added later, this filter will need to be updated to skip tombstoned comments.

## Implementation
Add the filtering logic to `apply_common_filters` in src/dogcat/cli/_helpers.py (around line 256), alongside the existing agent_only/manual_only filters. Then thread two new `typer.Option` parameters through every list-style command:

- `dcat list` (src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py)
- `dcat ready` (src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py)
- `dcat blocked` (src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py)
- `dcat pr` (src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py)
- `dcat snoozed` (src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_workflow.py)
- `dcat search` (src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_search.py)
- `dcat stale` (src/dogcat/cli/_cmd_stale.py)
- Any other commands in _cmd_workflow.py that already accept --agent-only (audit by searching for 'agent_only: bool = typer.Option')

Inbox proposals (`dcat inbox list`) do not have comments and should be skipped.

## Acceptance
- Both flags work on every command listed above
- Passing both flags errors out with a clear message
- Tab completion is unaffected (these are boolean flags, no value completion needed)
- Tests cover: has-comments matches issues with comments, without-comments matches issues without, mutual-exclusion error, and combination with other filters (e.g. --agent-only --has-comments)
acceptance:
- --has-comments and --without-comments flags added to all list-style commands (list, ready, blocked, pr, snoozed, search, stale, plus any other --agent-only-bearing commands)
- Filter logic implemented once in apply_common_filters
- Mutual exclusion enforced with a clear error
- Tests added for both flags including combined-with-other-filter cases
- just lint-all passes

Fix N+1 lookup patterns in deps.py
description: Five functions in deps.py repeatedly call storage.get() and storage.get_dependencies() per issue inside loops/recursion, causing O(n*d) performance. Affected: _has_deferred_ancestor (line 49-60), get_ready_work blocker check (71-88), get_blocked_issues (108-123), detect_cycles DFS (152-180), would_create_cycle (232-242). Fix: pre-build lookup dicts (all_issues_by_id, dep_graph) once before loops; memoize recursive calls.
acceptance: All deps.py functions pre-build lookup structures. No storage.get() calls inside loops. Existing tests still pass. Performance improvement measurable with large issue sets.

Add exhaustiveness check for unknown record types in classify_record()
description:
classify_record() in src/dogcat/models.py defaults to 'issue' for any unrecognized record_type value. If a new record type is added in the future without updating the merge driver, those records get misclassified and merged with wrong semantics — potentially causing silent data loss during merges.

Fix: Add an exhaustiveness check that warns or raises on unknown record types instead of silently defaulting to 'issue'. The merge driver (src/dogcat/merge_driver.py) relies on classify_record() at lines 207, 223, 251, 271, 297.

Files: src/dogcat/models.py:468-482, src/dogcat/merge_driver.py

Fix N+1 lookup patterns in deps.py
description: Five functions in deps.py repeatedly call storage.get() and storage.get_dependencies() per issue inside loops/recursion, causing O(n*d) performance. Affected: _has_deferred_ancestor (line 49-60), get_ready_work blocker check (71-88), get_blocked_issues (108-123), detect_cycles DFS (152-180), would_create_cycle (232-242). Fix: pre-build lookup dicts (all_issues_by_id, dep_graph) once before loops; memoize recursive calls.
acceptance: All deps.py functions pre-build lookup structures. No storage.get() calls inside loops. Existing tests still pass. Performance improvement measurable with large issue sets.
closed_reason: Pre-built lookup dicts in deps.py to remove N+1 patterns
